# Gemini — Project Configuration

## Identity and Purpose

`claude-config` is the source-of-truth repository for a global AI assistant configuration.
It defines a skill catalog, a memory layer, and a structured development workflow (SDD —
Specification-Driven Development). The repo deploys to a runtime directory via `install.sh`;
memory is captured back via `sync.sh`.

---

## Tech Stack

| Category | Technology |
|----------|------------|
| Language | Markdown + YAML + Bash |
| Framework | Claude Code SDD meta-system |
| Entry point | SKILL.md per skill directory |
| Package manager | N/A (skill files, not code) |
| Version control | Git |
| Hooks runtime | Node.js |

### Directory Structure

```
claude-config/
├── CLAUDE.md              # Orchestrator instructions
├── settings.json          # User settings
├── install.sh             # Deploy repo to ~/.claude/
├── sync.sh                # Capture memory/ from ~/.claude/
├── skills/                # Skill catalog (~47 skills)
│   ├── sdd-*/             # SDD phase skills
│   ├── project-*/         # Meta-tools
│   ├── memory-*/          # Memory management
│   └── [tech-skills]/     # Technology catalog
├── docs/adr/              # Architecture Decision Records
├── docs/templates/        # prd-template.md, adr-template.md
├── hooks/                 # Event hooks
├── openspec/              # SDD change artifacts
├── ai-context/            # Project memory layer
└── memory/                # Auto-memory
```

---

## Architecture

### Deployment Model

```
claude-config (repo)  ──install.sh──►  ~/.claude/ (runtime)
                       ◄──sync.sh────  (memory/ only)
```

- `install.sh` is repo-authoritative: copies everything repo → `~/.claude/`.
- `sync.sh` captures `memory/` only: `~/.claude/memory/ → repo/memory/`.
- **Never edit `~/.claude/` directly** — always edit in the repo.

### Skill Architecture

Every skill is a directory with a single `SKILL.md` entry point. Skills are categorized by
function:

| Category | Count | Examples |
|----------|-------|---------|
| SDD phases | 8+ | sdd-propose, sdd-spec, sdd-design, sdd-tasks, sdd-apply, sdd-verify, sdd-archive |
| Meta-tools | 10 | project-setup, project-audit, project-fix, memory-init, memory-update |
| Tech — Frontend | 8 | react-19, nextjs-15, typescript, zustand-5, tailwind-4 |
| Tech — Backend | 4 | django-drf, spring-boot-3, hexagonal-architecture-java, java-21 |
| Tech — Testing | 2 | playwright, pytest |

#### SKILL.md Format Type System

Every `SKILL.md` declares its structural type via the `format:` YAML frontmatter field:

| `format:` value | Required section | Used for |
|-----------------|-----------------|---------|
| `procedural` (default) | `## Process` | SDD phases, meta-tools |
| `reference` | `## Patterns` or `## Examples` | Technology skills |
| `anti-pattern` | `## Anti-patterns` | Anti-pattern catalog |

All formats require `**Triggers**` and `## Rules`.

### Inter-Skill Communication

Skills communicate via file artifacts — never via in-memory state. Key artifacts:

| Artifact | Producer | Consumer | Location |
|----------|----------|----------|----------|
| `audit-report.md` | project-audit | project-fix | `.claude/` in project |
| `analysis-report.md` | project-analyze | user | project root |
| `openspec/changes/*/tasks.md` | sdd-tasks | sdd-apply | `openspec/changes/<name>/` |
| `ai-context/*.md` | memory-init, memory-update | all skills | `ai-context/` in project |

### Key Architectural Decisions

1. **Skills are directories, not files** — allows co-locating templates and examples.
2. **SKILL.md is the convention** — exactly one entry point per skill directory.
3. **Artifacts over in-memory state** — skills pass data via files.
4. **Repo is authoritative** — config flows repo → `~/.claude/` only (except `memory/`).
5. **Two-tier skill placement** — global (`~/.claude/skills/`) or project-local (`.claude/skills/`).
   Project-local skills must be committed to the repo and are not excluded by `.gitignore`.
6. **Orchestrator delegates everything** — the global config never executes work itself; it launches
   specialized sub-agents that read individual SKILL.md files and do the work.

---

## Development Workflow

Changes to this project follow a structured cycle: **Propose → Spec + Design (parallel) → Tasks →
Apply → Verify → Archive**. This ensures every change has a written proposal, functional spec,
technical design, and task plan before any implementation begins.

The fast-forward shortcut (propose → spec+design → tasks in sequence) is the minimum required for
any skill change. Full cycle (including explore and verify) is required for breaking changes to
core orchestration skills.

**After implementation:**
1. Deploy the config to the runtime directory.
2. Commit the change with a conventional commit message.
3. Verify the result before archiving.

**Minimum for any skill change:**
```
propose  →  spec + design (parallel)  →  tasks  →  apply  →  deploy  →  commit
```

**For breaking changes to orchestrator or SDD phase skills:** full cycle is required.

---

## Conventions

### Language

**ALL content MUST be in English.** Skill files, YAML, docs, commit messages — no exceptions.
Spanish or any other language is a violation.

### Naming

| Element | Convention | Example |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Skill directories | kebab-case | `project-audit/`, `sdd-propose/` |
| Skill entry point | UPPER | `SKILL.md` |
| SDD phase skills | `sdd-[phase]` prefix | `sdd-propose`, `sdd-apply` |
| Meta-tool skills | `project-[action]` prefix | `project-audit`, `project-fix` |
| Tech skills | `[tech]-[version]` | `react-19`, `nextjs-15` |
| SDD change names | kebab-case descriptive | `improve-project-audit` |
| Archived changes | `YYYY-MM-DD-[name]` | `2026-02-23-add-project-fix` |

### SKILL.md Structure

```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: >
  One-line description.
format: procedural   # valid values: procedural | reference | anti-pattern
---
```

Sections (in order): H1 heading → blockquote → `**Triggers**` → format-specific main section
→ `## Rules` (always last).

### Git Conventions

- Conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `docs:`, `chore:` — always in English.
- **Config change workflow**: edit in repo → deploy → commit.
- **Memory capture workflow**: sync → `git add memory/` → commit.
- Commit after each SDD phase (at minimum after apply and after archive).

### ADR Convention

- Location: `docs/adr/NNN-short-title.md` (zero-padded 3-digit number, kebab-case title).
- Template: `docs/templates/adr-template.md` — Nygard format (Title, Status, Context, Decision,
  Consequences).
- Valid statuses: Proposed, Accepted, Deprecated, Superseded.
- `docs/adr/README.md` must be updated with every new ADR.
- Write an ADR for: new skill architecture patterns, install/sync direction changes, inter-skill
  communication convention changes, or any decision future sessions need to understand.

### PRD Convention

PRDs are optional for purely technical changes but recommended for user-facing or product-level
changes. A PRD precedes the proposal and feeds into it — it defines "what and why"; the proposal
captures "what and how." Template: `docs/templates/prd-template.md`.

---

## Working Principles

- **Clean and readable** over clever.
- **No over-engineering** — only what the current task requires.
- **No obvious comments** — only where logic is not self-evident.
- **Error handling at system boundaries** — user input and external APIs only.
- **No speculative features** or backwards-compatibility hacks.
- **Tests as first-class citizens.**

---

## Project Memory Layer

`ai-context/` is the project memory layer. Read relevant files at session start; update after
significant work.

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `stack.md` | Tech stack, versions, key tools |
| `architecture.md` | Architecture decisions and rationale |
| `conventions.md` | Code conventions, naming, patterns |
| `known-issues.md` | Known bugs, gotchas, limitations |
| `changelog-ai.md` | Log of AI-made changes |
| `ai-context/features/*.md` | Feature-level domain knowledge per bounded context |

---

## Known Issues & Gotchas

### CRLF Line Endings on Windows

Shell scripts may emit `command not found` errors due to CRLF. `.gitattributes` forces LF for
`.sh`, `.js`, `.md`. Fix on a new clone:
```bash
sed -i 's/\r$//' sync.sh install.sh
```

### rsync Not Available on Windows

`sync.sh` uses `rsync` which is not installed by default. Use `cp -r` manually as a workaround.

### GITHUB_TOKEN Dependency

`install.sh` registers the GitHub MCP server using `${GITHUB_TOKEN}`. Set this as a system
environment variable before running `install.sh`.

### Skills Modified in Session Must Be Synced

If a session modifies `~/.claude/skills/` directly, run `sync.sh` before the next deploy or those
changes will be overwritten.

### ai-context/ Marker-Awareness Gap

`project-analyze` writes `[auto-updated]` markers in `ai-context/` files. Other skills
(`memory-update`, `project-update`) are not aware of these markers and may overlap them. Risk is
theoretical — not observed in practice.
